inlineContent.text: To convert to absolute coordinates use the methods that take a size and return absolute values.
An absolute space can be any 2-dimensional space with a size, e.g. an image.

abstract.text: This class represents a 4-polygon, a polygon with 4 edges. The polygons points are stored in a normalized unit coordinate system from {0, 0} to {1, 1}.
Determines if the polygon is valid.
Clips the receiver the unit space {0, 0} - {1, 1}.
Helper method to transform an absolute point to the normalized unit coordinate system with given size.
One of the designated initializer methods.
Returns the absolute edge at the given index.
Checks if the polygon is convex by verifying that all cross products have the same sign.
Performs times clockwise rotations on the receiver.
